title: Ground Lattice Orbital-Restoring Simulation
native_dimension: 2D triangular lattice with a state-derived rendered curvature surface
core_rule: The visible lattice depression, sampled displacement height, and restoring gradient must come from the same evolving triangular-lattice height field; unequal restoring forces across an asymmetric bounded shell may generate orbit and axial torque; camera and projection transforms must not alter physics.
limitations: Curvature well is imposed, not derived.
Bounded displacement shell is a reduced coordinate, not a quark or particle.
Reference results validate code behavior only.
rendering_invariants: Triangular faces are shaded from actual node positions and heights.
Curved contours and depth marker sample the same interpolated lattice surface.
Vertical exaggeration and top-down view are projection-only.
The bounded displacement follows the same surface used for force calculation.
claim_gate_detail: YELLOW (reduced coupled simulation; physical derivation open)
